items.title: How one man's stolen identity was used to run a cult

children.content: In mid-2009 British citizen Simon Kadwill-Kelly was catching a ferry back from France. But as he tried to board, he learned his identity had been stolen — by the man accused of running an international cult from Nannup.

items.title: Killer of Perth woman Sharon Fulton sentenced to life in prison

children.content: A man who murdered his wife in Perth 40 years ago has been sentenced to life in prison with a minimum of 20 years. Due to his age and health issues, he is likely to die in custody.

image.altText: A dated image of a happy smiling woman.

items.title: 'Breaking point': Ex-greyhound racing insider speaks out as inquiry begins

children.content: A former greyhound racing insider tells how he reached "breaking point" with the industry, as a parliamentary inquiry into the sport in WA begins.

image.altText: A close up of a greyhound with a hand stroking under its chin.

items.title: Twenty people winched to safety in WA floods after drivers ignore road closures

children.content: Authorities say motorists are repeatedly ignoring warnings not to drive in floodwaters in WA's north in the wake of Cyclone Narelle, with 20 people having to be rescued.

items.title: Flooding leaves grieving boys stranded in Exmouth after father's death

children.content: Visitors in Exmouth who were stuck there when Cyclone Narelle passed are now trying to find ways out of the town, but that is proving impossible for one grieving family.

image.altText: A cardboard on a cafe bench with handwriting on it

items.title: Apocalyptic scenes from Australia ahead of cyclone make worldwide news

children.content: Cyclone Narelle turned the sky over parts of Western Australia's north into an ominous red, sparking headlines across the globe.

image.altText: The streets of shark bay are cast in an eerie cloud of red due to TC Narelle

items.title: Transphobia nearly killed Kaz. Now he just wants to be 'me'

children.content: This WA teenager was depressed, ill, and missing out on school. He says it was part of the toll transphobia took on him.

image.altText: A boy stands in front of ocean scenery.

items.title: Easter warning from WA police as South West road toll climbs

children.content: With 16 deaths on South West roads so far this year, police are pleading with holiday drivers to take a cautious approach.

image.altText: A blur of cars behind a 90km/h sign

items.title: Exmouth beaches a 'graveyard' after ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle

children.content: In the popular tourist destination, it appears the category four cyclone has chewed up and spat out whole sections of the Ningaloo Reef onto dry land.

items.title: From Porepunkah to Thologolong: The long search for Dezi Freeman

children.content: When the parents of Senior Constable Vadim de Waart-Hottart flew to Melbourne for a police service, little did they know their son's killer would emerge just days later.

items.title: Vaping likely to cause cancer, new Australian review of evidence finds

children.content: Nicotine-based vapes are likely to cause lung and oral cancer, according to a new review of evidence. Experts say there needs to be tougher enforcement of vape laws in Australia.

image.altText: young person with smoke coming out of their mouth after vaping

items.title: Antarctic penguin species appears on WA beach in rare sighting

children.content: The king penguin, known for being the second-largest penguin species and living on ice-free parts of the sub-Antarctic, was rescued near Esperance on WA's south coast.

image.altText: Sub-Antarctic king penguin found on WA's south coast in rare sighting

items.title: How supermarket chicken could get cheaper because of grain breakthrough

children.content: Australian researchers develop a high-protein sorghum variety, hoping to replace expensive feed grains in livestock systems that could mean cheaper meat and eggs.

items.title: What happens if your house is underinsured?

children.content: If your home is underinsured, it means your insurance policy won't fully cover the cost of rebuilding, repairing or replacing it.

items.title: Draft report into ANU's controversial cuts critical of university's council

children.content: ANU's council approved a massive $250 million cost-cutting program without clear evidence it was needed or achievable, according to an assessment in a draft audit office report.

items.title: Confronting images of 'unprecedented' outback flood after cyclone

children.content: An outback cattle station and tourist ranch in WA's north has felt the full force of Cyclone Narelle, with vision emerging of the trail of destruction left by the powerful wind and heavy rain.

image.altText: What appears to be a goat seeks shelter in a car while being pummelled with fast moving flood water.

articles.title: Emergency relief announced as cleanup begins for ex-tropical Cyclone Narelle

children.content: Structures have been destroyed and more than 1000 people remain without power as the cleanup begins in Western Australia following ex-tropical cyclone Narelle.

image.altText: A worker is repairing electricity transmission lines.

articles.title: Cyclone Narelle destroys banana crops, fresh food growers hit hard

children.content: Fruit and vegetable producers in one of Western Australia's key food growing regions are counting the cost of Cyclone Narelle, with one banana grower reporting more than 80 per cent of his crop has been destroyed.

items.title: 'You are just an immigrant': Millions in unpaid wages, modern slavery uncovered

children.content: Millions in stolen wages, threats of visa cancellation and modern slavery by Australian employers have been uncovered in the first year of a national program.

items.title: Italy's World Cup fate to be decided, Socceroos to learn final group opponent

children.content: The last of the 48 teams for the FIFA World Cup will be decided tomorrow, with Italy hoping to end a 12-year exile, while Türkiye and Kosovo look to join the Socceroos' group.

children.content: A stash of more than 800 mysterious drawings has been revealed for the first time, rewriting the timeline of Aboriginal art and reconnecting families with long-lost relatives.

items.title: Vital Aboriginal records saved in the 70s by an astute WA archivist

children.content: Margaret Medcalf, who turns 100 this week, insisted that decades of Aboriginal welfare records be preserved in the state archives and not destroyed. Those documents have proved essential to connecting Aboriginal people with their family history.
